sources.writer_profile: Paula Hardy is a Marrakesh-based British travel and food writer with over two decades of Morocco expertise, contributing regularly to Time Out and Lonely Planet guides. Sally Kirby is a writer based in Taghazout Bay, Morocco, bringing coastal and urban Moroccan dining perspective to her reviews. Both writers have documented firsthand dining experience across Marrakesh neighbourhoods; their editorial stance is staff-commissioned content for Time Out, which does not disclose whether meals are paid.

Nessrine Ben Moussa is a Moroccan travel consultant and blogger with twenty years of expertise, co-founder of Marrakech in Morocco, a site dedicated to local insights about the city. As a Moroccan national writing in English for an international audience, she brings an insider perspective on the Marrakesh dining scene across traditional and contemporary venues. Her editorial stance appears independent with no disclosed sponsorships, though her site also operates as a travel consultancy.

Jean Mayol is a French travel journalist writing for Nos Voyages Heureux, a French-language travel publication covering luxury and cultural travel destinations. His May 2025 Marrakesh piece focuses on the city's fine dining circuit, reflecting a visitor perspective from France with an emphasis on prestigious addresses rather than local everyday dining. His editorial stance is that of an independent travel journalist; no paid content disclosures are noted.

Gérard Flamme is a critic writing for Luxe Infinity Maroc, a Moroccan luxury lifestyle and gastronomy publication with French-language content focused on Morocco's high-end dining and hotel scene. His December 2025 review of Dardar Rooftop reflects embedded knowledge of the Marrakesh medina dining scene and the city's all-female brigade restaurant movement. The publication appears editorially independent in its restaurant coverage, though meal payment practices are not disclosed.

Valérie Desforges, known as Miss K, is a French luxury lifestyle blogger who has covered Morocco's gastronomic scene including the Royal Mansour Collection since before 2025. Her February 2025 article focuses on La Liste's recognition of Royal Mansour restaurants, providing a France-based food media perspective on Marrakesh's internationally acclaimed dining. Her editorial stance is that of an independent blogger; she does not disclose whether visits are hosted.

restaurants.name: Nomad
restaurants.signature_dish: Atlas honey panna cotta; orange-ginger-cardamom cake
restaurants.quote: Probably the medina's buzziest dining venue, with a towering rooftop in constant high demand; contemporary Moroccan cuisine attracting visitors from around the world.

restaurants.name: Dar Yacout
restaurants.signature_dish: Multi-course diffa feast with live music
restaurants.quote: 20 years on it has lost none of its glamour — one of the first Medina addresses to offer a fixed-price, multi-course gastronomic Moroccan diffa.

restaurants.name: Dardar Rooftop
restaurants.signature_dish: Cactus couscous; chicken pastilla with almonds; trout carpaccio with lemon and caviar
restaurants.quote: Perched on the rooftops of Marrakech's medina, Dardar imposes itself as an unmissable address — an entirely female brigade preparing cuisine that is precise and without flourishes, deeply rooted in contemporary Morocco.

restaurants.name: La Grande Table Marocaine
name_variants: La Grande Table Marocaine Royal Mansour Marrakech
restaurants.signature_dish: Friday couscous in vegetarian, chicken or lamb versions
restaurants.quote: Ranked 6th worldwide by La Liste 2025 with 97 points, it reinterprets ancestral traditions with audacious creativity and exceptional refinement — une cuisine raffinée, sublimée par un décor somptueux.

restaurants.name: Sahbi Sahbi
restaurants.signature_dish: Lamb tagine with aubergine and sweet sesame; pigeon pastilla
restaurants.quote: Food here is deeply traditional but executed in sophisticated style by some very experienced cooks.

restaurants.name: Farmers
restaurants.signature_dish: Squash blossom tacos; seasonal farm produce from permaculture farm
restaurants.quote: We love this brand-new spot in Gueliz for its cosiness, its very nice service and its seasonal, creative farm-to-fork menu.

restaurants.name: Mizaan
restaurants.signature_dish: Kefta; labneh; harissa and dark chocolate tapas platters
restaurants.quote: The tapas-style sharing platter menu is Moroccan meets Mediterranean with kefta, labneh and vibrant salads.

restaurants.signature_dish: Marinated sardines; black rice and crab croquettes; schnitzel
restaurants.quote: Simple dishes with sophisticated, punchy flavours made from organic, market-fresh ingredients.

restaurants.name: Amal Centre
restaurants.quote: Likely the best home-cooked meal you'll eat in Marrakech — Friday lunch is usually busy and advance booking is highly recommended.

restaurants.name: Comptoir Darna
restaurants.signature_dish: Lamb tagines; seafood platters; creative mezze
restaurants.quote: Lively venue combining Moroccan dining with evening entertainment, featuring live shows and an upstairs lounge for cocktails.

restaurants.name: Le Foundouk
restaurants.quote: Sunset is the perfect time to enjoy the breathtaking views from this rooftop combining traditional architecture with modern design in the medina.

restaurants.name: Palais Soleiman
restaurants.quote: A magnificently restored 19th-century palace immerses you in the Moroccan art of living through 100% Moroccan cuisine, with zellige tilework and carved ceilings. [Translated from French]

restaurants.name: SABO
restaurants.signature_dish: Daurade carpaccio with dill; lobster rigatoni in bisque sauce; wild squid carbonara
restaurants.quote: Michelin-starred chef Jean-François Piège creates a refined menu blending French expertise with the richness of Moroccan produce, opened May 2025 at the Selman Marrakech. [Translated from French]

restaurants.name: Sesamo
restaurants.quote: Ranked 25th worldwide by La Liste 2025 with 87.5 points, offering generous, creative and refined cuisine combining simplicity of flavours with the audacity of modern techniques. [Translated from French]
